title: Freshdesk Agent Analyzer v9
description: Evaluate support agent quality using five scoring dimensions, language/grammar QA, team-level feedback, and ticket metadata extraction from a full conversation transcript.

analysis_context.description: Context flags that help interpret the scores.

ticket_was_merged.description: True when the transcript explicitly indicates this ticket was merged into another ticket or thread.

merge_only_interaction.description: True when the transcript is mostly merge/system-routing activity with little or no agent-customer handling to evaluate.

ticket_metadata.description: Structured ticket details extracted only from explicit transcript evidence.

ticket_started_date.description: Original ticket open time from the transcript. Use the exact date-time string found in the transcript. If unavailable, return null.

ticket_closed_date.description: Last touch / close time from the transcript. Use the exact date-time string found in the transcript. If unavailable, return null.

agents_who_worked_on_ticket.description: First names of all support agents who handled the ticket based on explicit evidence. Return an empty array if none are identifiable.

customer_name.description: Customer first name only when explicitly present in the transcript. If unavailable, return null.

number_of_interactions.description: Count of interaction turns in the transcript.

problem_resolved.description: 1 = issue unresolved or mishandled, 5 = issue clearly resolved with no meaningful loose ends

tone_and_empathy.description: 1 = cold/dismissive, 5 = consistently warm, respectful, and empathetic

clarity.description: 1 = confusing or hard to follow, 5 = clear, concise, and easy to understand

grammar_and_spelling.description: 1 = frequent errors that harm readability, 5 = polished and error-free writing in agent messages. Ignore redaction artifacts containing the token 'censored'. Do not penalize long paragraph length alone when line breaks/HTML have been normalized away. Do not treat factual mistakes (for example wrong city/state/ZIP or incorrect order facts) as grammar errors.

overall_quality.description: Holistic quality score. Not a strict average. Weight problem_resolved most heavily.

language_and_grammar_feedback.description: Grammar/spelling feedback only for support-team messages, never customer messages. If there are no agent-side grammar/spelling issues, return null. Ignore artifacts introduced by data redaction/masking (for example tokens containing 'censored', including malformed email/web fragments like 'shop@:censored' or 'w::censored'). Do not give feedback solely because paragraphs are long when formatting was flattened by preprocessing. Exclude factual correctness issues (for example wrong city/state/ZIP/order facts) from this field.

overall_feedback_for_customer_support_te.description: Actionable team-level feedback in 1-3 sentences. Focus on what should be improved. Include material factual/detail errors from agent replies (for example wrong city/state/ZIP/order facts) when present. If no meaningful improvement is needed, return null.
